purpose: Report LOCATED, verbatim-anchored candidate AGD move observations for an argument-shaped nonfiction passage: each performative move a pluggable LLM judge identifies — ASSURING (authority/certainty in place of support), GUARDING (a claim weakened to shrink its commitment), DISCOUNTING (an objection anticipated and set aside, including cue-free structural dismissal) — with its family, verbatim span, paragraph index, and surface cue (null = cue-free). Identification is FUNCTIONAL (at transitions; cues are evidence, never criteria), aligned with the consumer audit's Layer-1 discipline (apodictic AGD Move Audit; R4A ADR D5: the producer observes, the consumer alone assigns codes). All three families are LEGITIMATE moves: an observation is a location, never a finding, code, or score; emits no adjudication, no aggregate-as-quality (Sinnott-Armstrong & Fogelin 9e, ch. 3).
use_when: You want a candidate inventory of assuring/guarding/discounting moves (with spans and paragraph loci) to seed or cross-check apodictic's AGD Move Audit Layer 1
Target is an argument-shaped nonfiction passage (op-ed / policy / testimony)
You want cue-free functional moves surfaced, not a cue-lexicon grep
do_not_use_when: You want a smuggling / soundness / quality adjudication or any diagnostic code (the surface refuses ALL of these — every observation is a legitimate move's location; the consumer audit alone challenges and codes)
You want to read observation COUNT as a quality signal (it is location data)
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed observation manifest

compute.cost_note: $0.01–0.10 per doc via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini (one labeling call); free with --judge manifest or --judge mock (a deterministic test stub); key-free with --judge agent_host (delegates to the host runtime's model; see spec 35)

examples.description: Located AGD move scan with a pre-computed observation manifest
